verses.text: James writes to the scattered tribes: 'count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations,' for trial works patience. Joy in trial. Testing matures faith into perfect work.
James writes to the scattered tribes: 'count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations,' for trial works patience. Joy in trial. Testing matures faith into perfect work.

James writes to the scattered tribes: 'count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations,' for trial works patience. Joy in trial. Testing matures faith into perfect work.
If any lack wisdom, 'let him ask of God,' who gives liberally; but let him ask in faith, for 'a double minded man is unstable.' Ask believing. Wavering prayer receives nothing.

Let the lowly rejoice in his exaltation, the rich in his lowliness, for he 'shall pass away as the flower.' Fading riches. Wealth withers; humble faith endures.
Let the lowly rejoice in his exaltation, the rich in his lowliness, for he 'shall pass away as the flower.' Fading riches. Wealth withers; humble faith endures.
Let the lowly rejoice in his exaltation, the rich in his lowliness, for he 'shall pass away as the flower.' Fading riches. Wealth withers; humble faith endures.
'Blessed is the man that endureth temptation,' for he shall receive 'the crown of life.' Crowned. Endurance under trial wins God's promised reward.
God tempts no man; each is drawn by 'his own lust,' which conceives sin, and sin brings forth death. Sin's path. Desire indulged ripens into death.

'Every good gift... cometh down from the Father of lights,' who 'of his own will begat us with the word of truth.' Good Giver. All blessing flows unchanging from God.
'Every good gift... cometh down from the Father of lights,' who 'of his own will begat us with the word of truth.' Good Giver. All blessing flows unchanging from God.

Be 'swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath'; lay aside filthiness and 'receive with meekness the engrafted word.' Listen first. The implanted word saves the humble soul.

Be 'swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath'; lay aside filthiness and 'receive with meekness the engrafted word.' Listen first. The implanted word saves the humble soul.
Be 'doers of the word, and not hearers only,' deceiving yourselves; the hearer-only forgets, but the doer is blessed in his deed. Do it. The mirror of the word must change the life.

Pure religion bridles the tongue, visits 'the fatherless and widows,' and keeps 'unspotted from the world.' True religion. Faith shows in mercy and clean living.
